paper_focus.title: Effects of annealing temperature on microstructure and mechanical properties of a low density δ-TRIP steel plate via intercritical quenching-annealing process
paper_focus.research_objective: To clarify how intercritical annealing temperature alters the laminated microstructure, austenite stability and TRIP/TWIP-assisted deformation behavior, thereby determining the achievable strength–ductility combination in a low-density Fe–9Mn–5Al–0.3C δ-TRIP steel.
material_systems: retained austenite (fine grains in tempered martensite)
main_process_variables: Intercritical quenching at 1050 °C for 1 h (water quench)
Annealing after quenching at 620, 680, 740, 800 °C for 1 h
